s1.text: My manager Priya said I had to come.

s3.text: I've been ordering dinner to my desk.

s4.text: Last Tuesday I didn't leave until the cleaners came in.

s5.text: I'm the lead on the rebrand.

s6.text: Then Marcus left, and they decided not to replace him.

s7.text: I could have said no. I didn't.

lines.text: I don't know why I'm here, exactly.
My manager Priya said I had to come.

choices.text: Tell me about the late nights.
So you're not here by choice.

lines.text: It's nothing dramatic. The deadline got moved up. Twice.
I've been ordering dinner to my desk.
Last Tuesday I didn't leave until the cleaners came in.

choices.text: What are you working on that's so heavy?
Do you ever eat with anyone during the day?

lines.text: I'm the lead on the rebrand.
It was supposed to be a three-person team.
Then Marcus left, and they decided not to replace him.
I could have said no. I didn't.

choices.text: Sounds like you wanted to prove something.
That was a mistake on your part.

lines.text: Alex leans back. The shoulders drop half an inch.
"I didn't want to let them down. Even Marcus, and he's gone."
They look at you, properly, for the first time.
"Can we do this again next week?"
"Look, I didn't come here to be lectured."
They check their phone. They don't put it down.
The rest of the hour is very quiet.
